summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a vibrant 1970 European set, highlighted by a soaring Dark Star and strong Hard to Handle.
review: Both reviewers rave about the high‑quality soundboard source, noting its clarity despite occasional tuning bits between songs. The performance shines with a groove‑laden Dark Star, a fiery Hard to Handle, and solid contributions from Garcia, Weir and Phil, while a few mid‑set numbers are seen as merely passable.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive comments on both the musical performance and the soundboard recording; two detailed reviews give a consistent picture, yielding a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Fabulous, crisp mix with only minor “extra tuning” bits between songs; no significant drop‑outs or balance issues reported.

poor_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: A mellow first half gives way to jam‑heavy second set, culminating in a soaring Dark Star; transitions (e.g., Attics) are praised and the overall pacing feels deliberate but rewarding.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone grooves on Dark Star and he “picks up the pace” during Hard to Handle.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s vocals receive special praise, described as loving and vulnerable.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir delivers a nice Me and My Uncle and contributes solid rhythm work.
